_meta.description: Seed map of DICOM pixel-data decoders to known memory-safety CVEs. SEED LIST — verify and refresh against NVD (nvd.nist.gov) and CISA ICS-medical advisories before relying on it.
_meta.note: The decoder->library mapping is authoritative; the specific CVE lists are illustrative of each decoder's history and are not exhaustive.
_meta.audit_note: Every CVE id below is auditable at the NVD url above (substitute the id). DicomLock reports decoder EXPOSURE, never a proven exploit.

cves.desc: integer overflow -> heap buffer overflow in opj_t2_encode_packet
integer overflow -> heap buffer overflow in rdppm.c (libjpeg-turbo)

CharLS.note: JPEG-LS decoder; fewer public CVEs but still untrusted C++ parsing attacker-controlled data — audit-worthy.

OpenJPH.note: High-Throughput JPEG 2000 (HTJ2K, transfer syntaxes .201-.203) decoder. Newer C++ codec adopted by DICOM in 2021 — less battle-tested than OpenJPEG, little public fuzzing history. Treat as untrusted C++ parsing attacker-controlled data.

cves.desc: heap buffer overflow (illustrative of FFmpeg's history)

FFmpeg-class.note: Extensive CVE history across demuxers/decoders; a 16-year-old FFmpeg bug was surfaced by Anthropic's Claude Mythos in 2026.

zlib.note: Deflated Explicit VR LE (1.2.840.10008.1.2.1.99) routes the ENTIRE data set through zlib inflate before parsing — a broader exposure than per-pixel codecs.
